microsoft_ft: Owned **end-to-end release lifecycle** for Azure's resilience validation program, scaling from a 2-person operation to a platformized system sustaining 45+ annual drills and driving **$14M+ business impact** across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Directed **14 cross-org release executions** under executive visibility, serving as connective tissue across engineering, test & evaluation, and program management — including a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ contract**.
Built an internal AI agent and restructured release-planning workflows, cutting planning cycle time by **39%** and increasing release cadence capacity by 21% through data-driven process improvements.

Championed JIRA and Confluence adoption across release teams to centralize issue tracking, visualize **critical-path blockers**, and deliver real-time stakeholder transparency across concurrent high-priority programs.

Improved release documentation integrity by migrating to an AI-powered **semantic search system** with rigorous metadata standards, cutting artifact lookup time by 83% and reducing friction in cross-team release handoffs.

Defined a **release automation roadmap** after conducting discovery with 20+ service teams, quantifying 81 hours of manual toil per region launch and building the business case to automate critical deployment paths.

Facilitated Agile sprint ceremonies and **managed release backlog prioritization**, resolving 40+ high-priority tickets to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain deployment schedule integrity.

Drove cross-functional alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **CI/CD pipeline automation**, compressing the software deployment cycle by 25% and improving release stability for mission-critical robotics systems.
